pay_equity.disclaimer: Descriptive pay comparisons by gender and race/ethnicity for decision support only. These summaries do not control for job level, tenure, performance, location, or other legitimate factors. They are not legal pay equity or EEO compliance determinations. Groups with fewer than five employees are hidden to reduce re-identification risk.

tenure.disclaimer: Tenure summaries are based on hire date and current base salary. They do not adjust for job level, location, performance, or promotion history. Use for directional review only.

location_pay.disclaimer: Location pay comparisons are descriptive medians only. They do not control for job level, cost of living, tenure, or role mix. Groups with fewer than five employees are hidden.

merit_by_department.disclaimer: Department merit averages use uploaded merit increase values only. They do not adjust for level mix, performance, or eligibility rules.

outliers.reason: Bonus target 12.0% is unusually low for level 3 (level median 15.0%, expected 13.1%–16.1%).
Bonus target 12.0% is unusually low for level 3 (level median 15.0%, expected 13.1%–16.1%).
Bonus target 8.0% is unusually low for level 2 (level median 10.0%, expected 10.0%–10.0%).

bonus_target_review.disclaimer: Bonus target outliers compare each employee to others at the same job level. Levels with fewer than four employees are skipped.

post_merit_compa.disclaimer: Projected compa-ratio assumes the uploaded merit increase is applied to current base salary with no range changes. Use for planning only.

peer_spread.disclaimer: Peer spread flags groups with the same job level and department where pay spans more than 15%. Review role scope and recent hires before adjusting.

flags.reason: Merit 2.5% is below the Below 85% compa band guideline (3.0–6.0%).
Merit 18.0% exceeds the 85–95% compa band guideline (2.5–4.5%).

Merit 3.2% exceeds the 105–115% compa band guideline (0.0–2.5%).

merit_matrix.disclaimer: Merit matrix checks compare uploaded merit increases to default compa-ratio bands. Adjust guidelines in your comp policy as needed — these are directional QA flags only.

issues.description: 2 range width (16.2% of midpoint) is unusual compared to other levels.

range_structure.disclaimer: Range structure checks use the most common min/mid/max per job level in your file. Mixed ranges within a level may indicate mapping or grade assignment errors.

compa_penetration_summary.disclaimer: Aggregate compa and penetration by level and department for cycle readouts. Does not adjust for performance, tenure, or location.

total_cash_comp.disclaimer: Total cash comp = base salary + (base × bonus target %). TCC compa uses the same salary range midpoint as base compa unless your ranges are TTC-specific.

new_hire_placement.disclaimer: New hires hired within the last 365 days. Review placement at hire and whether starting pay still sits below range minimum.

geo_pay_policy.disclaimer: Include Pay Zone and Geo Differential columns to compare actual vs expected location pay adjustments.

currency_report.disclaimer: Include a Currency column for multi-currency workforce summaries.

employee_type_report.disclaimer: Include Employee Type to summarize workforce mix and exclude non-core populations.

midpoint_progression.disclaimer: Midpoint progression checks assume job levels sort low-to-high. Non-numeric level names use keyword ordering when numbers are absent.

review_queue.disclaimer: Review queue prioritizes compensation issues for cycle readiness. Critical items should be addressed before finalizing merit recommendations.

merit_budget_variance.disclaimer: Compare your target merit % (in Overview) to the file average and department pools. Positive variance means uploaded merit exceeds your target envelope.

performance_merit.disclaimer: Map a Performance Rating column to check merit vs performance alignment.

executive_summary.headline: 13 compensation issues flagged for review.
bullets: This analysis covers 18 employees. 2 rows were excluded due to incomplete data.
3 employees are below range minimum, requiring $11,000 to reach the range floor.
1 employee exceeds their range maximum by a combined $2,000.
1 duplicate employee ID was found in the source file.
4 salary compression patterns may create internal equity risk.
2 managers are paid below at least one direct report.
1 employee has an unusually high or low equity / LTI grant compared to the rest of the file.
2 employees have a merit increase that may not align with compa-ratio positioning — review under-correction and over-rewarding cases.
Average compa-ratio is 94.6% (3 employees below 90% of midpoint).
Uploaded merit data implies a projected merit pool of $76,156.
Merit by department: Operations averages 10.5% vs Product at 3.07%.
Average compa-ratio moves from 94.6% to 98.7% after uploaded merit increases (1 employees would remain below 90%).
6 peer pay spread flags — same level and department groups with more than 15% pay spread.
3 bonus target outliers vs. same job level.
6 merit increase flags outside default compa-ratio matrix bands.
5 range structure issues — invalid order, overlap, or unusual width.
Total cash comp (base + target bonus) averages $115,306 across employees with bonus targets.
Gender median pay gap: Male median is 8.9% above Female ($8,500 difference).
Race/ethnicity median pay gap: Asian median is 3.2% above White.

budget_impact.note: Total budget impact combines one-time pay adjustments to reach range minimums plus the projected annual merit pool from uploaded merit increase data.
